title: Manages embed definitions and their persistence in JSON files
purpose: Provides methods to create, read, list, and delete embed definitions stored as JSON files in a directory structure. Used to maintain a registry of embed configurations.

steps.content: Ensure the base directory exists for storing embed files.
Read all files from the base directory.
For each file ending in .json, attempt to parse and load its embed data.
IF parsing fails due to corruption, skip that file and continue with others.
IF the directory doesn't exist, initialize an empty index and continue.

methods.name: create
methods.normalized_params: (params: { name: string; url: string; subtype?: 'storybook'; width?: string; height?: string; storybook?: { storyId: string; port: number } }): Promise<Embed>

steps.content: Validate that the provided URL starts with http:// or https://.
Generate a unique ID for the embed based on its name, ensuring it's URL-safe.
IF the generated ID already exists, append a numeric suffix to make it unique.
Construct the embed object with the provided parameters and current timestamp.
Write the embed data to a JSON file in the base directory.
Update the in-memory index with the new embed metadata.
Return the created embed object.
Iterate through all entries in the in-memory index.
For each entry, retrieve the corresponding embed data from disk.
If retrieval succeeds, add the embed to the results list.
Sort the list of embeds by creation date in descending order.
Return the sorted list of embeds.
Look up the embed metadata in the in-memory index by ID.
IF no metadata is found, return null immediately.
ELSE read the embed data from the file path stored in the metadata.
IF reading fails due to file corruption or deletion, return null.
ELSE parse the file content as JSON and return the embed object.
Look up the embed metadata in the in-memory index by ID.
IF no metadata is found, throw an error indicating the embed was not found.
ELSE delete the corresponding file from disk.
Remove the embed entry from the in-memory index.
Check if the provided ID exists in the in-memory index.
Return true if the ID is found, false otherwise.
Return the total number of entries in the in-memory index.
Clear all entries from the in-memory index.
